storyInfo: The miners celebrate their brief victory, with the exception of the father who killed his own son, and two friends at odds with each other. In the researcher's recollection, it all began with the discovery of a new substance in an exhausted mine shaft.

attributes.content: At least eat something, Hawley.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Look, white bread. You don't see it much outside the cities.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: *sigh*...
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: <p=1>Far North Tundra, Ursus</><p=2>Three days ago...</>
attributes.name: group_location_stamp

attributes.content: Huh?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: They've started singing...
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Nikto! Found you at last.
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: Come on, Nikto, oddball, city-dweller, professor and cynic. Let's dance. It's just the right kind of music.
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: Now's not the time for dancing.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: The bonfire is going to draw attention in this darkness.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: But we found a tractor and a bunch of supplies. Not much, but enough to get us further away from the blasted place. Isn't that worth celebrating?
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: Unchipped, out of the mines, beat the Patrol Units, and now we have a tractor and supplies. It's beyond my wildest dreams.
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: Where are Vetochki and Rankin?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: You're always looking for them. It's not like you're bound to each other. It's okay to give them a little breathing room, you know.
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: I do happen to know where Rankin is, though. Beside that bonfire that you don't like. Want to join us?
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: Last question. Who's playing the accordion?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: You'll find out, if you come with!
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: Nikto takes the gloveless hand offered to him. The palm is rough but warm, different from any other in those memories that he has intentionally forgotten.
They walk in the direction of the bonfire and music.
He...
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: I said he was here.
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: Yes, he is here.
Many times Nikto saw him clench his hands into fists, or swing a weapon.
Nikto could not imagine how those hands, knotted with scars and thick with veins, could operate something as delicate as an accordion.
He plays the accordion?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Yeah. What about it?
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: By the fireside, Rankin's callused fingers leap across the keyboard, as he gently pulls and presses the bellows.
Music fills the air.
See? I told you.
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: Come, don't just stand there. It's not like we get to relax every day!
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: She catches him like a hunter catches prey. As he is brought into the crowd, his feet instinctively begin to step in rhythm.
Nikto was once an excellent dance partner. He tried to stop thinking about 'once' when he came to the mining district.
Get that frown off your brow and everything off your mind. Just dance, let your body move and rid yourself of all your troubles.
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: The crowd moves and sings to the beat, responding to the rhythm with every joint in their bodies.
It is the simplest, most primitive form of dance.
...Hah.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Nikto exhales and begins to move, bending his knees, kicking and spinning, every movement animated and rhythmic.
I knew it! You can dance!
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: See? I figured no one could deny the energy here.
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: Nikto does not answer, continuing to kick and turn mechanically, unemotionally.
Step forward and step back, jump and turn, until the next downbeat.
He takes the hands of his dance partner, and begins to lead the dance.
Wow—
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: No need to thank me.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: No humility in you, is there? But...
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: It's clear that you're really good at dancing, even to an untrained eye. Your speed, and this 'magic' under your feet... it's almost like flying.
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: What do they call it? Pas? E-Enchainement?
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: Call it the pathetic instincts of an ornament.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: What do you mean—
attributes.name: Jolly Miner

attributes.content: There is no explanation. Stepping energetically in time, Nikto pulls his partner towards him, then pushes her in the opposite direction.
Laughing, she runs into a young man without a partner, but eager to try, and so Nikto lets go and hands her over to her new partner.
By the time the dance has ended, Nikto is standing very near Rankin. Take one step back, and he would be sitting by Rankin's side.
But Nikto does not do so. Before he opens his mouth, he reminds himself: Do not press. Ask Rankin about the truth calmly, gently. Do not cause a commotion.
Do not interrupt this rare moment of joy.
I didn't know you played the accordion.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: I didn't know you danced.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I guess we're not close enough to share everything.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: But there is something you must tell me, if you really consider me a friend.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: You went to see Hawley. Is he still not eating?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: What did you expect?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: We need to talk, Rankin...
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Rankin sighs, brings the melody to a close, and hands the accordion to a nearby miner.
Nikto recognizes the face of Pavel, Bogina's brother. Is Bogina still running a fever? He does not know.
Pavel takes the accordion and begins to sing an unfamiliar song.
Lively, soaring, yet sad.
'Sweet soft fragile flower, lean against the oak tree...'
attributes.name: Soft Singing

attributes.content: 'Hang not your head, nor sway with the wind...'
attributes.name: Soft Singing

attributes.content: (Hushed) Let's talk over there.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Rankin gets up, and they leave the crowd and commotion behind.
You didn't need to do that.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: You could have done it yourself, like you usually did. I would have praised you for your kindness, if you had sent Hawley away on some sort of pretext when you did the deed.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: But why did you make Hawley do it?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: 'Rankin the Noble.' 'Rankin the Hero.' Is it not enough that you've triumphed over the foe? Did you need to torture your own comrades like that?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Does it make you happy? Make your victory more glorious? Why did you—
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Because I was angry.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Yes... I won't make excuses, pretend that I expected Hawley to let his son go. This was what I wanted. I'm happy about it.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Should I be calm? Should I be merciful? End his pain like you said? Why should I?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Did you think the people who were loaded into their containers did not beg for mercy? Beg for someone to end their pain?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: You accuse me of being unkind. Who would accuse those beasts on their behalf?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I do not understand them. They look human and they speak the language of humans, but their deeds are more monstrous than any monster.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: How could they prey upon their own kind like that, so calmly, so coldly?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: This difference frightens me. It's as though... as though we never shared the same human dignity with them. If they're not the monsters... then it must be me who is the monster.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Rankin's words are clear, his tone impassive, as he gazes into the black night. Nikto wants to turn Rankin's head around and make him say it to Nikto's own face.
But he does nothing.
Silence.
But you were right. I didn't need to do that.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: It's pointless...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: You're torturing yourself, Rankin.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: You shouldn't pretend to be as strong as you think you are, and you shouldn't demand that others be as strong.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: It's not my place to accuse you. You carry the responsibility of everyone on your shoulders. You must become the hero. It's a remarkable thing.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: But you can't pretend that the fear and hesitation in your heart don't exist... you're trying to hide from yourself.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: The result is what happened today... you lost control. It's dangerous. You don't know what you might do, the next time your emotions get the better of you.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Thank you for the warning... I'll remember it.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Let's gather the others and put the bonfire out. It's time to go—how far until the next village?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: ...Let's wait a while longer.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: They're happy. They haven't been so happy for a long time. We found a vehicle, and we're one step closer to the Kazimierzian border... let them enjoy themselves a little longer, and warm themselves up a bit.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: You enjoyed yourself when you danced just now, didn't you?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: What about Hawley? Do you still care about how he's feeling?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: He won't eat anything, and he's not responding to people talking to him.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: He'll come around.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: You can keep thinking that way, if it makes you feel better.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Rankin turns around, takes a few steps towards the campfire, before stopping in his tracks. He turns back around and walks in the other direction, until he leaves behind the friend with whom he has developed differences, and the comrades still enjoying themselves.
He keeps walking until the scene of unfading crimson once again fills his thoughts.
What do I do, Rankin? I-I think my skull's crushed. There's blood everywhere—
attributes.name: Distant Voice

attributes.content: Help me, Rankin! I don't want to die!
attributes.name: Distant Voice

attributes.content: Rankin—
attributes.name: Distant Voice

attributes.content: Sorry.
attributes.name: Rankin?

attributes.content: Accept it.
attributes.name: Piercing Sound

attributes.content: Accept pain as you accept mortal pleasures. Accept death as you accept the life that Ursus grants.
attributes.name: Piercing Sound

attributes.content: Therein lies salvation.
attributes.name: Piercing Sound

attributes.content: Yes, I accept.
attributes.name: Rankin?

attributes.content: Who are you? Who the hell are you?!
attributes.name: Twisted Figure

attributes.content: Our souls could have been saved! This isn't how it should have been! It was you, abomination, that set us adrift, never to find salvation!
attributes.name: Twisted Figure

attributes.content: It's your fault—your fault that we have nowhere to go—
attributes.name: Twisted Figure

attributes.content: Abandon all hope.
attributes.name: Black Snow

attributes.content: All of you...
attributes.name: Black Snow

attributes.content: You never had anywhere to go.
attributes.name: Black Snow

attributes.content: (Panting heavily)
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: *pant*... *pant*...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Nadia... why are you here? Is Vetochki not with you?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I came to find you... Rankin.
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: Why... is there something I can help with?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: No... I would like to help you.
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: Oh...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: You killed that thing. It became your revelation, to carry the burden of all.
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: I understand your pain... it is painful to carry the burden of the life and death of others, to carry the burden of duty... how could it not be?
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: I witnessed how your soul became torn and tattered. But I do not have the cure. I could not compliment your courage, for it was the source of your pain...
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: Thank you for your understanding.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But there is no need to pity me. I chose this duty for myself.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Besides... no, I never believed in revelations. I've always done what I thought was right.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Yes. You chose it yourself. Poor boy... no one can save you, except yourself...
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: I can only send you my sincere blessings, that one day... one day, you will find salvation from pain, and the peace that you desired...
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: The old woman puts a withered hand on Rankin's chest, and he feels his chest filled with warmth.
I—
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Rankin touches his chest.
Was he too tired, or hallucinating?
He does not feel his heartbeat.

attributes.text: Since you asked, let's start with the crackdown in the mining district.
It began with people who had too much time on their hands, who discovered a new substance deep within an exhausted mine shaft.
Of course, we began to study it.

attributes.content: The Infected are one thing, but why can't we leave the mining district either?
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: There's nothing coming out of the mines. The miners are being shut inside their quarters, unable to go to work. Who knows if the Originium veins inside have really dried up?
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: Let's sit and wait. The Grand Duchess won't abandon us.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: Wait? What's the point of waiting? They're not issuing our salaries, just supplies. Why not just let us leave, then? Not everyone's so carefree like you. I've got a mother to provide for.
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: We've got news! The mines haven't been exhausted!
attributes.name: Excited Researcher

attributes.content: What? Really?
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: Guess what we found at the bottom of the mine?
attributes.name: Excited Researcher

attributes.content: The Originium's grown back?
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: No! You don't have any imagination.
attributes.name: Excited Researcher

attributes.content: Imagination leads to futile hope, which leads to death. I don't want to die yet.
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: So, what exactly did you find?
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: Geologists from Bresk say that it's a geological fault, not exhaustion! Faults cause shifts in rock formation, resulting in Originium ore seemingly disappearing, when in fact it's been transformed.
attributes.name: Excited Researcher

attributes.content: Can you explain it in a way that a non-geologist could understand?
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: Meaning that the Originium ore is gone, but something new has appeared.
attributes.name: Excited Researcher

attributes.content: Look at this! Fresh out of the hole. I'm taking it to the lab for analysis. Here's your chance to help!
attributes.name: Excited Researcher

attributes.content: The excited researcher takes out a flask of black liquid, the surface of which is smooth as a mirror, and shakes it gently.
Are you sure? I'm no geologist, but I don't think faults turn rock from solid to liquid...
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: Are you sure they didn't make it up just so that we'll have something to do?
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: I went down myself, alright? The stuff's everywhere. It's like an underground river. The miners said the Originium ore was gone, but it's only been transformed into this stuff.
attributes.name: Excited Researcher

attributes.content: You have a chance to be a co-author on my paper! You should be more grateful!
attributes.name: Excited Researcher

attributes.content: Fine, fine I'll come. Like I don't know you just wanted to use my lab.
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: And what about it? Yours is the tidiest...
attributes.name: Excited Researcher

attributes.text: Indeed. How could a fault turn Originium ore from solid to liquid?

But there was no need for us to doubt the truth that was plain as day. After swift analysis and comparing the results, we confirmed that it was a substance the likes of which we've never seen before, and we named it 'Oka'.
Then, the Grand Duchess' orders came.

attributes.content: Go straight into human testing? Who ordered it, the director?
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: No, not even on the director's orders. We don't understand its nature, and we haven't even begun animal testing or energy testing. We're being too hasty. I'm going to find him—
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: There's no point talking to the director. The volunteer quota has already been filled, and Lakoda is moving forward with the tests.
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: Besides, this comes directly from the Grand Duchess. She wants results in two weeks.
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: Two weeks? That's not even enough to find out its half-life.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: I don't like this. I—
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: If you don't like it, you can leave the Oka research.
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: I was in charge of extracting the active component of Oka, and I worked with Lakoda to develop the animal testing reagent. Of course I want to continue!
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: I just—I just don't think this is right, to go straight to human testing with zero idea about what might happen.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: The sooner we get results, the sooner the Grand Duchess lets me go home. That's my only concern right now.
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: Like I told you, not everyone is as carefree as you are.
attributes.name: Worried Researcher

attributes.content: But—
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.text: The human experiments began regardless. I was excluded and did not have access to the experiment data.
I wanted to figure out the nature of Oka and why it appeared in the mines. So...
The experiments on lab musbeasts showed no progress. At my wits' end, I even thought about experimenting on myself.
I never did, of course, because I learned that those so-called volunteers were not volunteers after all.

attributes.content: You need to get out of here, Ksenia.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: Serafina? How did you get in here? I thought there were guards at the door. Are there new volunteers from the miners—
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: Listen to me, Ksenia. Those miners never volunteered!
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: Even the patients who were in our medical post were forcibly conscripted. Your colleague was such a smooth liar that I didn't detect she was lying at first, when she said they volunteered.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: What?
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: All those 'volunteers' who participated in the experiments never volunteered! They never came back! Whatever's happening here goes against all scientific ethics.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: You remember Vetochki? The bell toller, the one who stammers a little?
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: Good girl. She remembers everyone in the mining district, and she told Rankin, the foreman, as soon as she noticed that people had gone missing.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: First, the Infected miners, then their non-Infected families...
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: Rankin went missing for a while too, reappearing with the bodies of a few miners who had gone missing.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: Gosudar-batyushka... so when Lakoda said that the experiments were paused because of 'equipment theft'...
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: I don't know the details about your situation, but the miners have left their quarters and are coming to the lab. They want an explanation.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: The miners are mostly Infected, and they have the strength of numbers. The guards can't stop them if they're determined to force the issue. There will be blood.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: But... but I've never heard about any volunteers showing rejection symptoms, much less death—
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: Are you sure?
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: I... no, I'm not.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: Do you have the experiment data?
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: No. I've been looking for the director all day to request access, but I haven't been able to find him.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: I don't know what you're researching here. Ultimately, the worst that can happen to the Infected is to be incinerated.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: If this place still retains the function of a hospital, as His Imperial Majesty hoped, you can check the incinerator or morgue.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: After that, you can come to the medical team's camp if you want to come with me.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.text: Clothing without owners was stacked haphazardly in the basement. The incinerator was still running. The last person keeping watch over the place had left, leaving behind bodies that had not yet disintegrated scattered like the clothes.
The volunteers who participated in the experiment died en masse in the span of just two weeks. The impact of Oka on the human body was beyond doubt.

attributes.content: <p=1>Central Far North Mining District Laboratory, Ursus</>
attributes.name: group_location_stamp

attributes.content: As was the case with the human experiments, I was excluded from the evacuation.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: The lab was empty by the time I went to the basement, learned the truth, and put all the still-intact bodies in cold storage.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: In fact, I can only guess that there was an evacuation. I don't know where they went—the director, Lakoda, or anyone else.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: Thinking back on it, it was just like Chernobog, except this time I was the one in the lab.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: You were too young at the time, and you were on the project as a student intern. You were not on the list of core staff.
attributes.name: Mon3tr

attributes.content: H-How did you know?
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: Were you—
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.options: We found 'Dominion' pollution in the mining district.

attributes.content: The Royal Guard was here. Not anymore.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.options: Before you passed out, you said that Oka could kill a Royal Guard.;Then you said the miners killed a Royal Guard.
Which one is true? Or are they both true?

attributes.content: (Mutters) ...Chyort.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: I don't think there's anything to talk about.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: How does an ordinary person who was born and raised in Ursus describe the sudden collapse of the violence that had dominated her life for so long?
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: The Royal Guards are the stuff of nightmares to the children of Ursus. They watch your every word and action, so you are cautious and careful, so you are well-behaved and obedient.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: They are invincible, undefeatable.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: When you suddenly witness the death of one... it only means that something even more terrible has appeared.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.options: The miners touched by Oka killed the Royal Guard.

attributes.content: No, no... I don't know anything about it. Forget I ever said anything.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: I didn't see anything, and you didn't hear anything. Just leave.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.content: I've told you everything you wanted to know, everything that I can tell you.
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.options: Even if all it does is accelerate the deaths of the Infected...;It would be an unimaginable disaster if it got out.
Leave aside the question of who killed the Royal Guard, or even if anyone killed him at all.;We don't know if the fleeing Infected miners came into contact with the substance.

attributes.content: If they did, and it is contagious, they would not have allowed the researchers to leave.
attributes.name: Mon3tr

attributes.content: The changes brought about by Oka are not necessarily contagious. At least, based on the experiment data that I managed to find, they haven't begun comparative observation yet—
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.options: Do you still have a sample of Oka?

attributes.content: No... What would you intend to do with it?
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.options: It's not about what we intend to do. It's about what we can do.
We'll have to get our hands on a sample to find out.

attributes.content: But all the samples are... you're going into the mines?! Now?
attributes.name: Ksenia

attributes.options: It seems to be the only option.
Don't you want to find out what Oka really is?
